perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video starts with a young Korean man in a black jacket and a black scarf standing on a sidewalk at night. He is looking at the camera with a curious expression. (2) As he speaks, a pop-up image of soy milk and fried dough sticks appears at the bottom of the screen, followed by his transition into a wide smile. (3) A second man wearing a blue puffer jacket enters the frame from the left. Both men stand together, putting their hands together in a prayer-like gesture of gratitude. (4) The two men laugh, wave at the camera, and repeatedly bow slightly while continuing to smile brightly against the backdrop of a brightly lit storefront.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) A male voice starts speaking in a mix of Korean and Mandarin with a friendly tone. There is light ambient street noise. (2) An upbeat, medium-tempo electronic background music track enters as the man says 'Toh-sia' (Hokkien for thank you). (3) Sound effects like a subtle 'ding' occur when text/images appear. (4) Two male voices are heard laughing and repeating the phrase 'Toh-sia' several times until the audio fades out.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Hook text at the top: 'POV 剛學台語的韓國歐巴' (POV: Korean Oppa who just learned Taiwanese Hokkien) with a South Korean flag emoji. (2) Functional subtitles: '你說說剛學到的台語吧！' (Tell me the Taiwanese you just learned!) and '我可是有學到一句！' (I actually learned one sentence!). (3) Decorative/Pun text: '豆漿？...' (Soy milk?...) with a food image, followed by '原來是「多謝」' (Turns out it was 'thank you'). (4) Closing hook: '如果在台北看到韓國男人講多謝的話就是他們兩個囉' (If you see Korean men saying thank you in Taipei, it's these two).

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro -> Language Demo -> Pun reveal -> Duo interaction/Outro

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main creator in black, 1 friend in blue joining later.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Two stylish young Korean men; one in a black leather jacket and scarf, the other in a bright blue puffer jacket.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A nighttime city street in Taipei with illuminated storefronts, a red poster on a glass door, and a street light casting a warm glow.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Natural night lighting with artificial store lights

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is a lighthearted cultural vlog intended to entertain by showing the humorous side of Korean people learning Taiwanese Hokkien. It uses a phonetic pun between 'Toh-sia' (thank you) and 'Dou-jiang' (soy milk) to create a comedic moment, followed by a wholesome interaction between friends.

social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The use of 'Oppa' in the title and the focus on linguistic puns makes this highly relatable to a Taiwanese audience interested in Korean culture.
